chunk_summary: Transition planning from pediatric to adult obesity services involves early preparation, addressing lifestyle changes, and ensuring continuity of care, especially for adolescents on pharmacotherapy or post-bariatric surgery.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of early transition planning (12–24 months prior) for adolescents with obesity.
Learn key components of a transition checklist, including documentation and self-management training.
Identify strategies to address lifestyle changes and adherence challenges during transitions.
Recognize the specific needs of post-bariatric surgery adolescents, including lifelong micronutrient monitoring.
key_facts: Transition discussions should begin 12–24 months before transfer to adult care.
A transition checklist includes identifying an adult provider, follow-up schedule, and a written summary of medical history and treatment.
Adolescents on injectable agents should be trained in self-administration, storage, and refill planning.
Post-bariatric surgery adolescents require lifelong micronutrient monitoring and pregnancy counseling for post-menarcheal females.
Screening for depression and disordered eating is critical during transitions to ensure adherence.
reference_quote: Start transition discussions 12–24 months before the expected transfer to adult care, especially for adolescents on pharmacotherapy or after bariatric surgery. (AAP Clinical Practice Guideline, 2023)
document_key_topics: Transition planning for adolescent obesity care
Lifestyle and adherence challenges during transitions
